title: The chestnut sweeper
summary: An old street sweeper in Lisbon cleans the square every morning. He does not sweep one thing. A boy asks him why.
body:
Mr Antonio sweeps the square in Lisbon every morning. He starts at six o'clock. The square is small. There are four benches and one big tree. The tree has many chestnuts on the ground.

Mr Antonio sweeps the leaves. He sweeps the paper. He sweeps the dust. But he does not sweep the chestnuts. He leaves them on the ground.

A new boy comes to the square. He looks at the chestnuts. He says, 'Mr Antonio, you forgot the chestnuts.'

Mr Antonio smiles. He says, 'I did not forget. The squirrels eat the chestnuts. If I sweep them, the squirrels are hungry.'

The boy looks at the tree. He sees one squirrel. He sees two squirrels. He sees five squirrels. They eat the chestnuts on the ground.

'How long do you do this?' the boy asks.
'Twenty-six years,' says Mr Antonio. 'My father swept this square before me. He also left the chestnuts.'

The boy nods. He learns something today. The square looks clean for people. It is not clean for squirrels.

Mr Antonio finishes his work at seven. He sits on a bench. He drinks his coffee. The squirrels are still busy in the tree.

annotations.meaningVi: đã quên (quá khứ bất quy tắc của 'forget')
đã quét (quá khứ bất quy tắc của 'sweep')
để chúng lại, không đụng đến chúng ('leave' = để lại, không lấy đi)
gật đầu (đồng ý hoặc hiểu ra điều gì đó)
trước tôi, trước khi tôi làm (chỉ thứ tự thời gian hoặc kế thừa)

grammarNotes.title: Present simple for daily routines
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Bài dùng thì hiện tại đơn để kể những việc lặp lại mỗi ngày. 'Mr Antonio sweeps the square every morning.' 'He starts at six o'clock.' 'The squirrels eat the chestnuts.' Với chủ ngữ số ít (he/she/it) ta thêm '-s' vào động từ. Câu phủ định dùng 'does not + động từ nguyên thể': 'He does not sweep the chestnuts.'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the present simple for repeated actions. The verb takes -s after he/she/it: 'Mr Antonio sweeps the square.' The negative is 'does not + base verb': 'He does not sweep the chestnuts.' Time phrases like 'every morning' and 'at six o'clock' often signal the present simple.

translationVi:
Ông Antonio quét quảng trường ở Lisbon mỗi sáng. Ông bắt đầu lúc sáu giờ. Quảng trường nhỏ. Có bốn cái ghế dài và một cây to. Trên mặt đất dưới cây có rất nhiều hạt dẻ.

Ông Antonio quét lá. Ông quét giấy. Ông quét bụi. Nhưng ông không quét hạt dẻ. Ông để chúng lại trên mặt đất.

Một cậu bé mới đến quảng trường. Cậu nhìn những hạt dẻ. Cậu nói: 'Ông Antonio ơi, ông quên hạt dẻ rồi.'

Ông Antonio mỉm cười. Ông nói: 'Tôi không quên đâu. Mấy con sóc ăn hạt dẻ. Nếu tôi quét đi, sóc sẽ đói.'

Cậu bé nhìn lên cây. Cậu thấy một con sóc. Cậu thấy hai con sóc. Cậu thấy năm con sóc. Chúng đang ăn hạt dẻ trên mặt đất.

'Ông làm việc này bao lâu rồi ạ?' cậu bé hỏi.
'Hai mươi sáu năm,' ông Antonio nói. 'Cha tôi từng quét quảng trường này trước tôi. Ông cũng để lại hạt dẻ.'

Cậu bé gật đầu. Hôm nay cậu học được một điều. Quảng trường nhìn sạch với người. Nhưng nó không sạch với sóc.

Ông Antonio xong việc lúc bảy giờ. Ông ngồi xuống một cái ghế. Ông uống cà phê. Lũ sóc vẫn còn bận rộn trên cây.

questions.prompt: What does Mr Antonio do every morning?
questions.explanation: 'Mr Antonio sweeps the square in Lisbon every morning.'
questions.hint: Look at the very first sentence.

questions.prompt: What time does Mr Antonio start work?
What does Mr Antonio NOT sweep?
questions.explanation: 'But he does not sweep the chestnuts.'
questions.hint: Paragraph 2 lists what he does sweep, and then one thing he does not.

questions.prompt: Why does Mr Antonio leave the chestnuts on the ground?
options: The squirrels eat them, and he does not want them to be hungry.
questions.explanation: 'The squirrels eat the chestnuts. If I sweep them, the squirrels are hungry.'
questions.hint: Mr Antonio's first long answer to the boy.

questions.prompt: How many squirrels does the boy see in the tree?
questions.explanation: 'He sees one squirrel. He sees two squirrels. He sees five squirrels.'
questions.hint: The numbers go up step by step.

questions.prompt: How many years has Mr Antonio done this work?
questions.hint: Mr Antonio gives the exact number when the boy asks.

questions.prompt: Who swept the square before Mr Antonio?
questions.explanation: 'My father swept this square before me.'
questions.hint: Mr Antonio mentions a family member.

questions.prompt: What does the boy learn today?
options: That the square is for people only.
That a clean place can be clean for some and not for others.
That Mr Antonio works too slowly.
questions.explanation: 'The square looks clean for people. It is not clean for squirrels.'
questions.hint: Look at the line just after the boy nods.

questions.prompt: What does Mr Antonio do after he finishes his work?
options: He sits on a bench and drinks coffee.
questions.explanation: 'He sits on a bench. He drinks his coffee.'

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: A small daily choice can be kind to a quiet neighbour you cannot see.
Old jobs are better than new ones.
questions.explanation: Mr Antonio sweeps for people but quietly feeds the squirrels by leaving the chestnuts.
questions.hint: Think about who Mr Antonio is helping, on top of the people.

blanks.explanation: He sweeps every morning, starting at six.
One big tree stands in the square.
He sweeps the dust along with leaves and paper.
The boy thinks he forgot the chestnuts.
If he sweeps the chestnuts, the squirrels are hungry.
He counts up to five squirrels.
He drinks his coffee on the bench.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Mr Antonio cleans for people but leaves food for animals he never speaks to. Is there a small daily act in your life that helps someone — or something — you never thank or get thanked by? Would you keep doing it if no one ever noticed?
